name: Avocado Soup with Radishes and Cilantro Cream
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Walnut Oil or 3 tablespoons Olive Oil
1 piece Ginger, 1 1/2 inches long, peeled and minced
3 Serrano or jalapeno chili peppers, stemmed and seeded, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat avocado oil or olive oil in a saucepan and add chopped onion. Saute for 5 minutes.
2. Add garlic, ginger, and 2 of the chili peppers. Saute for another 5 minutes.
3. Add chicken stock and simmer for 10 minutes. Remove from heat.
4. While simmering, cut avocados in half, seed, and peel them.
5. Chop the avocados and stir with lime juice. Place about half the avocado mixture into a blender container.
6. Add hot stock and vegetables until the container is nearly full and process until smooth and liquid.
7. Chill the soup thoroughly for several hours.
8. To make cilantro cream, mix sour cream and half-and-half. Stir in chopped cilantro, remaining chili pepper, ground coriander, and salt to taste. Chill until ready to use.
9. Cut radishes into julienne strips and toss with walnut oil or remaining olive oil, lime juice, and salt to taste. Chill until ready to serve.
10. Taste the chilled soup and add salt and more lime juice as needed.
11. Serve the soup in bowls, topped with a tablespoon of cilantro cream and a tablespoon of julienned radishes.
Variation: This soup is also delicious when served hot and makes an ideal starter for a hearty Mexican meal.
